run_metadata.notes: Threat model: Penny’s dashboard API is gated by a shared secret, Supabase JWT, or HMAC session cookie (apps/dashboard/proxy.ts). The Python repair API requires REPAIR_SERVICE_SECRET. Primary residual risks are (1) forged inbound webhooks if verification is incomplete, (2) shared-secret tenancy (any authenticated caller can act on any project name), and (3) XSS stealing secrets stored in browser storage. Analysis stopped early after confirming a webhook auth bypass (LYRA stop condition).

agent.name: security-and-privacy-auditor
agent.role: Identify practical security and privacy risks with evidence-backed findings and remediation.
stop_conditions_hit: critical_auth_bypass: GitHub webhook HMAC not verified (LYRA: stop further analysis)

files_skipped: apps/worker/** — spot-checked grep only; no full pass after webhook blocker
Full Supabase RLS policies — not reviewed in this run
coverage.incomplete_reason: Stopped after P0 webhook verification gap per agent-security.md stop condition; did not exhaust all API routes or worker paths.

findings.title: GitHub webhook accepts any signature header without HMAC verification
findings.description: The Supabase Edge function for GitHub webhooks rejects requests in production only when the x-hub-signature-256 header is missing. It never computes HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET or compares it to the header. An attacker who discovers the deployed function URL can send POST requests with a dummy x-hub-signature-256 value and trigger audit_runs inserts using the service role client, causing unauthorized queue load and potential cost/DoS.

proof_hooks.summary: Production only requires presence of signature header; no HMAC verify implementation before processing body.
Illustrative attack shape against a deployed endpoint (verify legally on your own stack only).
steps: Obtain the public URL of the github-webhook function.
POST JSON mimicking a push event with header x-hub-signature-256: sha256=fake.
Observe that the request is not rejected for bad MAC (only missing header fails in production).

repro_steps: Deploy or run the function with NODE_ENV=production.
POST valid JSON with x-github-event: push and x-hub-signature-256: sha256=00 (non-empty).
Confirm handler proceeds past the signature check without cryptographic verification.
findings.impact: Unauthorized job enqueue into audit_runs, compute spend, and database writes as the Supabase service role. Same shared secret cannot mitigate inbound spoofing because verification is absent.

suggested_fix.approach: Verify GitHub's HMAC-SHA256: read raw body, compute HMAC with GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET, timing-safe compare to x-hub-signature-256 (strip sha256= prefix). Reject on mismatch. Use raw body before JSON parse.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Ensure the handler uses the raw request body for HMAC; parsing JSON first can break signature verification.
tests_needed: Unit test: valid signature passes; wrong secret fails; tampered body fails.
Integration: GitHub delivery with real signature in staging.

findings.title: Orchestration enqueue secret can be read from sessionStorage by page scripts
findings.description: apiFetchWithEnqueueSecret merges a user-supplied enqueue secret from sessionStorage into Authorization headers. Any XSS in the dashboard origin can read sessionStorage and exfiltrate the secret, extending compromise beyond httpOnly cookie session theft.

proof_hooks.summary: Session storage value is attached as Bearer token when present.

findings.impact: A successful XSS can steal the shared enqueue secret and replay API calls until rotation.

suggested_fix.approach: Prefer httpOnly cookie-only auth for orchestration; if a second factor is required, use short-lived CSRF-protected forms or subresource isolation. Minimize secret lifetime in sessionStorage.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Changing auth UX may affect operators who paste secrets into the UI.

next_actions.action: Implement HMAC-SHA256 verification for GitHub webhooks using raw body and GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET; deploy and regression-test.
next_actions.rationale: P0 blocker: prevents forged webhook traffic and abuse of service-role inserts.

next_actions.action: After webhook fix, run a focused pass on remaining apps/dashboard/app/api/** routes for IDOR patterns if multi-tenant hosting is planned.
next_actions.rationale: Shared-secret model does not separate projects by principal; confirm product intent.

next_actions.action: Evaluate reducing reliance on sessionStorage for enqueue secrets or scope to memory with explicit user re-entry per session.
next_actions.rationale: Reduces XSS blast radius for orchestration credentials.
